steps.title: Apply the equation from the given condition
steps.content: Divide by $4$ and square: $\sqrt{2x}=4$ gives $2x=16$, so $x=8$.

steps.title: Compute the target value
steps.content: Then $6x = 6 \cdot 8 = 48$. That matches choice B — B is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice B is correct. Dividing each side of the given equation by $4$ yields $\sqrt{2x}=4$. Squaring both sides of this equation yields $2x=16$. Multiplying each side of this equation by $3$ yields $6x=48$. Therefore, the value of $6x$ is $48$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A is incorrect. This is the value of $3x$, not $6x$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice C is incorrect. This is the value of $9x$, not $6x$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice D is incorrect. This is the value of $12x$, not $6x$.
